question: Looking at the US unemployment rate map, which statement below is the most accurate?

options.A: Maine and Rhode Island have similar unemployment rates.
options.B: Arizona and Maine may differ in unemployment rate, even though they fall into the same color range.
options.C: All states shown have nearly identical unemployment rates.
options.D: The unemployment rate differences are impossible to determine from this visualization.

explanation: Maine (8.2%) and Rhode Island (9.9%) have a 1.7% unemployment rate difference, while Illinois (7.7%) and Arizona (8.4%) have only a 0.7% difference. However, the categorical color encoding groups Maine and Rhode Island into the same color bin (8-10% range), making their large difference appear similar, while Illinois and Arizona appear more different due to different color bins.
